meaning: adv.性情乖僻地,酸酸地,坏心眼地
examples: "Leave my mother out of it," he said sourly.
"Nice try, Aunt Dede," Lainey replied sourly from the back seat.
The shopworkers' union, and retailers' associations which opposed the liberalisation, are sounding sourly triumphant.
The old soda bottles, lottery tickets and newspaper clippings had them sourly perplexed.
